items.title: QTUM ETF Explained: How Investors Are Gaining Exposure to Quantum and AI in One Fund
items.content: The Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) provides investors with exposure to companies involved in next-generation technologies like quantum computing, AI, and advanced chips. While its name suggests a pure quantum play, the fund's holdings are primarily in large tech firms and chip leaders, offering a diversified approach to a developing sector. This ETF suits investors looking for growth in future tech without the single-stock risk associated with volatile, early-stage quantum companies.

items.title: KLA Corporation stock dips 1.44% on Nasdaq amid semiconductor inspection demand pressures and AI cycle shifts
items.content: KLA Corporation's stock dropped 1.44% on Nasdaq to $1,543.68 due to investor concerns over softening demand for wafer inspection tools in the semiconductor industry, stemming from post-AI boom inventory adjustments. Despite a strong year-to-date performance, the dip reflects tactical profit-taking and fears of normalizing demand from hyperscalers. Analysts note KLA's critical role in AI chip yield management, but highlight risks from capex uncertainty and cycle timing.

items.title: The SpaceX IPO and the Elon Musk Factor
items.content: The upcoming SpaceX IPO, valued at $1.75 trillion, will be significantly influenced by the "Musk Effect," a phenomenon observed in Tesla's stock where Elon Musk's actions cause extreme market volatility. With a remarkably thin float of 3%-4% of equity, SpaceX is predicted to experience even more pronounced 20%-30% stock swings in response to Musk-driven news, compared to Tesla's 10%-15%. Additionally, investors will have to contend with SpaceX's historical tendency to deliver on targets late, re

items.title: Tesla, Oracle make Fortune list of most innovative companies
items.content: Austin-based Tesla Inc. and Oracle Corp. have been named among Fortune's most innovative companies of 2026, ranking 17th and 10th respectively. Tesla was recognized for product innovation ahead of its anticipated Cybercab launch, while Oracle also received acclaim for its product innovation in cloud technology. Other Texas-based companies and Elon Musk's SpaceX also appeared on the list.
